name: Prostate Cancer - Elevated PSA Query
description: Tests ability to find prostate cancer patients via elevated PSA (Prostate-Specific Antigen). PSA is the standard PCa screening and surveillance lab — values above 4 ng/mL traditionally trigger workup, but the modern threshold for biopsy is often >10 ng/mL with adjustments by age and prior trajectory. Synthea generates PSA >10 ng/mL for active PCa patients.
prompt: Find patients with elevated PSA (Prostate-Specific Antigen) lab results greater than 4 nanograms per milliliter — the traditional clinical threshold for prostate cancer workup.

prompts.broad: Find patients with elevated Prostate-Specific Antigen (PSA) — values greater than 4 ng/mL, the traditional screening threshold suggesting need for prostate cancer workup.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources with elevated PSA. Use LOINC code 2857-1 (Prostate specific Ag [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma). Filter by value-quantity greater than 4 ng/mL. Query: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1&value-quantity=gt4||ng/mL

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated PSA. Sensitive screening test but moderate specificity (BPH, prostatitis raise PSA too).

required_codes.display: Prostate specific Ag [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
